lessons.subject: Life Orientation
lessons.topic: Development of the self in society
lessons.subtopic: Development of the self in society
lessons.title: Grade 7 Development of the self in society — Core teaching
learning_objectives: Explain core concepts related to development of the self in society.
Use CAPS enquiry, practical, creative or source-work skills for the topic.
Apply the topic in familiar South African classroom and everyday contexts.
lessons.lesson_body:
In this lesson learners study development of the self in society using clear classroom examples. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to development of the self in society. Key vocabulary: Development, self, society. Discuss personal development, health choices and responsible relationships using the example scenario. Grounding snippet: --- page 13 ---
LIFE ORIENTATION GRADES 7-9
8
CURRICULUM AND ASSESSMENT POLICY STATEMENT (CAPS)
SECTION 2: INTRODUCTION TO LIFE ORIENTATION
2.1 WHat is liFe orientation?
Life Orientation is central to the holistic develo Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

8
CURRICULUM AND ASSESSMENT POLICY STATEMENT (CAPS)

2.1 WHat is liFe orientation?
Life Orientation is central to the holistic develo Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: Example 1: How can a Grade 7 learner show respect while learning about development of the self in society?
step_by_step_solution: Name the situation in the example.
Identify a respectful action.
Explain why the action helps the group.
Write the answer in one clear sentence.
worked_examples.answer: Listen to others, take turns and use kind words during the activity.

worked_examples.question: Example 2: Which healthy choice supports the lesson on development of the self in society?
step_by_step_solution: Read each option carefully.
Eliminate choices that ignore safety or wellbeing.
Select the option that supports healthy habits.
Explain the choice to a partner.
worked_examples.answer: Wash hands, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice shows a responsible action during a lesson on development of the self in society?

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.B: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
options.C: Copy another learner without understanding
options.D: Leave the activity before it is completed

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps a learner reflect on development of the self in society?

options.A: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.B: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.C: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of development of the self in society?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.C: Ignore feedback from the teacher
options.D: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for development of the self in society and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about development of the self in society and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.LO.1.1 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect development of the self in society to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for development of the self in society. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: life orientation.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about development of the self in society without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports life orientation learning at home.

options.B: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.C: Why should I skip checking my work?
options.D: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of constitutional rights and responsibilities?

options.A: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.B: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.C: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When procedure without conceptual understanding appears, return to the worked example for constitutional rights and responsibilities and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about constitutional rights and responsibilities and teach it to a partner using the visual approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.LO.2.2 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect constitutional rights and responsibilities to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for constitutional rights and responsibilities. Use the visual variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: life orientation.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about constitutional rights and responsibilities without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports life orientation learning at home.

options.B: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.C: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of constitutional rights and responsibilities?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.C: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When procedure without conceptual understanding appears, return to the worked example for constitutional rights and responsibilities and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about constitutional rights and responsibilities and teach it to a partner using the visual approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.LO.2.2 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect constitutional rights and responsibilities to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for constitutional rights and responsibilities. Use the visual variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: life orientation.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about constitutional rights and responsibilities without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports life orientation learning at home.

step_by_step_solution: Read each option carefully.

worked_examples.answer: Wash hands, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure.

worked_examples.question: Example 1: How can a Grade 7 learner show respect while learning about study skills?
step_by_step_solution: Name the situation in the example.
Identify a respectful action.
Explain why the action helps the group.
Write the answer in one clear sentence.
worked_examples.answer: Listen to others, take turns and use kind words during the activity.

worked_examples.question: Example 2: Which healthy choice supports the lesson on study skills?
step_by_step_solution: Read each option carefully.
Eliminate choices that ignore safety or wellbeing.
Select the option that supports healthy habits.
Explain the choice to a partner.
worked_examples.answer: Wash hands, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice shows a responsible action during a lesson on study skills?

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.B: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
options.C: Copy another learner without understanding
options.D: Leave the activity before it is completed

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps a learner reflect on study skills?

options.A: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.B: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.C: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of study skills?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.C: Ignore feedback from the teacher
options.D: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for study skills and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about study skills and teach it to a partner using the real world sa approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.LO.2.4 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect study skills to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for study skills. Use the real world sa variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: life orientation.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about study skills without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports life orientation learning at home.

worked_examples.question: Example 1: How can a Grade 7 learner show respect while learning about health, social and environmental responsibility?
step_by_step_solution: Name the situation in the example.
Identify a respectful action.
Explain why the action helps the group.
Write the answer in one clear sentence.
worked_examples.answer: Listen to others, take turns and use kind words during the activity.

worked_examples.question: Example 2: Which healthy choice supports the lesson on health, social and environmental responsibility?
step_by_step_solution: Read each option carefully.
Eliminate choices that ignore safety or wellbeing.
Select the option that supports healthy habits.
Explain the choice to a partner.
worked_examples.answer: Wash hands, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice shows a responsible action during a lesson on health, social and environmental responsibility?

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.B: Copy another learner without understanding
options.C: Leave the activity before it is completed
options.D: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps a learner reflect on health, social and environmental responsibility?

options.A: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.B: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.C: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of health, social and environmental responsibility?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.C: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for health, social and environmental responsibility and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about health, social and environmental responsibility and teach it to a partner using the story approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.LO.3.1 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect health, social and environmental responsibility to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for health, social and environmental responsibility. Use the story variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: life orientation.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about health, social and environmental responsibility without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports life orientation learning at home.

learning_objectives: Explain core concepts related to career awareness.
Use CAPS enquiry, practical, creative or source-work skills for the topic.
Apply the topic in familiar South African classroom and everyday contexts.
lessons.lesson_body: Learners move through career awareness one guided step at a time with teacher checkpoints. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to career awareness. Key vocabulary: Career, awareness, reference, covers. Discuss personal development, health choices and responsible relationships using the example scenario. Grounding snippet: CAPS reference 7.LO.4.4 covers Career awareness. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.
lessons.explanation: Learners move through career awareness one guided step at a time with teacher checkpoints. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to career awareness. Key vocabulary: Career, awareness, reference, covers. Discuss personal development, health choices and responsible relationships using the example scenario. Grounding snippet: CAPS reference 7.LO.4.4 covers Career awareness. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: Example 1: How can a Grade 7 learner show respect while learning about career awareness?
step_by_step_solution: Name the situation in the example.
Identify a respectful action.
Explain why the action helps the group.
Write the answer in one clear sentence.
worked_examples.answer: Listen to others, take turns and use kind words during the activity.

worked_examples.question: Example 2: Which healthy choice supports the lesson on career awareness?
step_by_step_solution: Read each option carefully.
Eliminate choices that ignore safety or wellbeing.
Select the option that supports healthy habits.
Explain the choice to a partner.
worked_examples.answer: Wash hands, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice shows a responsible action during a lesson on career awareness?

options.A: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
options.B: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.C: Copy another learner without understanding
options.D: Leave the activity before it is completed

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps a learner reflect on career awareness?

options.A: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.B: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.C: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which extension task best deepens understanding of career awareness?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.C: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When procedure without conceptual understanding appears, return to the worked example for career awareness and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about career awareness and teach it to a partner using the step by step approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.LO.4.4 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect career awareness to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for career awareness. Use the step by step variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: life orientation.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about career awareness without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports life orientation learning at home.

source_citations.citation_text: CAPS reference 7.LO.4.4 covers Career awareness.
